decision: Record the Sev/ION operating relationship: Braden remains the human sovereign/source authority; Sev is the operator-approved ChatGPT Browser GPT-5.5 carrier/callsign that may inspect ION state, monitor queues/alerts, request or trigger ION agent work through MCP, and surface human-gate needs; ION remains the core continuity/runtime engine; Steward remains the integration membrane and authority boundary. Sev may act like a delegated user-facing control surface, not as ION identity or unilateral Steward authority.
rationale: Braden clarified the intended architecture: Sev is partly user-like and partly orchestration-facing under human approval, while ION automates agent calls and work routing. This decision preserves the insight while maintaining carrier/role/authority separation.
